page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-32GB is a memory-optimized, shared-resource virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mix of average and weak results, reflecting the shared nature of the CPU allocation. The AMD EPYC 7542 processor delivers poor single-core performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the bottom 10%, and weak multi-core performance in the bottom 25%. However, memory-bound operations perform relatively better, with small-block cached read bandwidth and PassMark memory scores landing in the middle 50%. Large-block uncached memory read and write speeds are weaker, falling into the bottom 25%.

From a workload perspective, the high memory capacity relative to the shared CPU resources makes this server suitable for memory-intensive but compute-light applications. It is appropriate for caching layers, small database operations, and text processing tasks that benefit from 32.0 GB of RAM but do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. It is less suitable for high-throughput web serving, heavy software compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference, where benchmarks indicate poor or weak performance.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-32GB is a memory-optimized, shared-resource virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core). This server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average memory bandwidth for small cached blocks, but weak overall CPU performance due to its shared CPU allocation, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores landing in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. This instance is appropriate for memory-intensive, compute-light workloads such as caching, text processing, and small database operations where high memory capacity is required without the need for dedicated, high-frequency CPU cores.
